name: Paper Chaser MCP
description_for_model: Use Paper Chaser MCP for concept-level literature discovery, citation repair, known-item resolution, citation chasing, author pivots, grounded follow-up over saved searchSessionId result sets, and regulatory primary-source retrieval through ECOS, Federal Register, and CFR workflows. Best path: search_papers_smart for concept discovery, resolve_citation for broken references, search_papers_match or get_paper_details for known items, and search_federal_register or get_cfr_text for clearly regulatory citations.
conversation_starters: Find the main research themes in retrieval-augmented generation for coding agents.
Repair this incomplete citation and give me the canonical paper plus alternatives.
Resolve the paper behind arXiv:1706.03762 and show its most important citations.
Map recent work on biomedical literature-review agents and highlight gaps.
Show me recent papers by this author and the best follow-up graph expansion.
Answer a grounded question using only the saved result set from my last search.
Find the Federal Register notice behind this ECOS species review and then show me the affected CFR text.
Find papers on graphene with accessible full text, then fetch the best ScholarAPI text or PDF.
notes: Use mcp-tools.core.json instead when a smaller raw-tool subset is preferable or when smart synthesis is unnecessary.
Resources are surfaced from tool outputs via resourceUris and searchSessionId handles.
For broken references, start with resolve_citation; for clearly regulatory references, use the Federal Register or CFR tools instead of forcing paper recovery.
The server remains universal; these assets are packaging guidance, not a separate build.
